description: Fix five concrete bugs in browser extension command handlers: stale page globals after executeScript timeout, incorrect visibility check for fixed/sticky elements in waitForElement, pre-navigation URL returned by navigateTab, false positive from deleteCookies on non-existent cookie, and orphaned setTimeout timers in Promise.race patterns.

userStories.title: Fix stale page globals after executeScript outer timeout
userStories.description: As a developer using browser.executeScript, I want page globals to be cleaned up even when the outer timeout fires, so that the next executeScript call on the same tab does not read stale results from the previous timed-out execution.
acceptanceCriteria: When SCRIPT_TIMEOUT_MS fires before EXEC_MAX_ASYNC_WAIT_MS, __lastExecResult and __lastExecAsync are deleted from the page via a best-effort chrome.scripting.executeScript cleanup call
The cleanup is wrapped in .catch(() => {}) so it does not throw if the tab was closed
A unit test verifies that the cleanup function is called when the outer timeout fires

userStories.title: Fix waitForElement visibility check for fixed/sticky elements
userStories.description: As a developer using browser.waitForElement with visible: true, I want the visibility check to correctly detect position: fixed and position: sticky elements, so that waiting for modals, sticky headers, and fixed navbars does not always time out.
acceptanceCriteria: The visibility check handles position: fixed and position: sticky elements (where offsetParent is null even though the element is visible)
The visibility check still correctly detects truly hidden elements (display: none, visibility: hidden, zero dimensions)
A unit test verifies that a position: fixed element is detected as visible
A unit test verifies that a position: sticky element is detected as visible
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/interaction-commands.ts, line 206.

The bug: Line 206 uses `htmlEl.offsetParent !== null` as the primary visibility check. offsetParent returns null for position: fixed and position: sticky elements, even when they are fully visible on screen. This means waitForElement with visible: true always times out (10 seconds) for these elements.

Fix approach: Replace the visibility check with one that handles all cases. A robust approach:

const style = getComputedStyle(htmlEl);
const isVisible = style.display !== 'none' && style.visibility !== 'hidden' && (htmlEl.offsetParent !== null || style.position === 'fixed' || style.position === 'sticky');

This preserves the existing offsetParent check for normal elements while correctly handling fixed/sticky. The getComputedStyle call for display !== 'none' is already on line 206, so this consolidates the two checks.

Note: The injected function runs in the page's MAIN world via chrome.scripting.executeScript, so standard DOM APIs are available.

userStories.title: Fix navigateTab returning pre-navigation URL
userStories.description: As a developer using browser.navigateTab, I want the response URL to reflect the navigation target, so that the MCP client can correctly confirm the navigation succeeded.
acceptanceCriteria: handleBrowserNavigateTab response URL is always the requested navigation URL, not the tab's pre-navigation URL
A unit test verifies the response contains the requested URL
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/tab-commands.ts, line 64.

The bug: chrome.tabs.update(tabId, { url }) returns the Tab object immediately, before navigation completes. The tab.url field contains the old (pre-navigation) URL. The fallback `?? url` never fires because the old URL is truthy (non-empty string).

Fix: Change line 64 from:
  sendSuccessResult(id, { id: tab?.id ?? tabId, title: tab?.title ?? '', url: tab?.url ?? url });
to:
  sendSuccessResult(id, { id: tab?.id ?? tabId, title: tab?.title ?? '', url });

Always return the requested URL since chrome.tabs.update succeeding means navigation was initiated. The title will also be stale, but there's no way to get the new title without waiting for onComplete — the empty string fallback is acceptable.

userStories.title: Fix deleteCookies false positive on non-existent cookie
userStories.description: As a developer using browser.deleteCookies, I want the response to accurately indicate whether a cookie was actually deleted, so that the MCP client does not falsely believe the cookie was removed.
acceptanceCriteria: When chrome.cookies.remove returns null (cookie not found), the response contains deleted: false
When chrome.cookies.remove returns a cookie details object (cookie deleted), the response contains deleted: true
A unit test verifies the deleted: false case

userStories.title: Fix orphaned setTimeout timers in Promise.race patterns
userStories.description: As a developer, I want setTimeout timers in Promise.race patterns to be cleaned up when the other branch resolves first, so that orphaned timers do not accumulate in the service worker.
acceptanceCriteria: handleExtensionGetSidePanel clears its timeout timer after Promise.race resolves
handleExtensionCheckAdapter clears its isReady timeout timer after Promise.race resolves for each tab
A unit test verifies timers are cleared in the happy path (non-timeout branch wins)
